verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B encodes the beta subunit of the mitochondrial F1-ATPase catalytic core. It is a soluble matrix-facing subunit assembled into the F1 headgroup on the matrix side of the inner mitochondrial membrane. It has no transmembrane domain, no GPI anchor, and no signal peptide directing it to the cell surface under baseline conditions. Checking contextual buckets: (1) cell_state_induced — some papers report ectopic surface ATP synthase on tumor/endothelial cells, but this is controversial and not wash-resistantly anchored via a known mechanism; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — no documented lineage-specific surface display; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — not a lysosomal membrane protein; (4) dual_localization — proposed 'ectopic' surface ATP synthase on cancer cells exists in literature, but this is debated and no TM anchor mechanism is defined for ATP5F1B itself; (5) stable_surface_attachment — no covalent or wash-resistant anchoring to a PM partner demonstrated for this subunit. The ectopic surface ATPase reports are insufficient evidence for a confident contextual call; the dominant localization is mitochondrial matrix/inner membrane facing matrix.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Some literature reports ectopic surface display of F1-ATPase beta subunit on tumor and endothelial cells (angiostatin receptor context), which could support a 'contextual/cell_state_induced' call, but mechanistic evidence for PM anchoring is lacking.
